concerns.text: FR-004 and SC-002 explicitly mandate 'Pearson correlation coefficients' between CV and dataset properties. Task T021 in the plan prioritizes 'Spearman Rank Correlations' as the primary test and relegates Pearson to secondary. The tasks.md lacks a specific task to implement the mandated Pearson correlation calculation, risking non-compliance with the functional requirement.

FR-007 requires multiple-comparison correction to control the 'family-wise error rate' (FWER). Task T026 describes applying Benjamini-Hochberg (FDR) to 'control the family-wise error rate'. This is a statistical contradiction (BH controls FDR, not FWER). There is no task explicitly implementing a FWER-controlling method (e.g., Bonferroni) or correcting the terminology to match the implemented FDR method, leaving the requirement ambiguous.

FR-005 mandates a Permutation Test on 'absolute differences of squared deviations'. Task T025 describes the test as comparing the 'variance of |Acc_A - Acc_B|' (absolute differences). The statistical definition of the test statistic differs between the spec and the task description. No task explicitly implements the 'squared deviations' variant required by FR-005.

SC-005 requires measuring the 'family-wise error rate' against the target alpha level. Task T028 generates a report confirming the 'methodology' was applied but lacks a specific task to calculate or verify the *achieved* FWER in the output, leaving the success criterion unmeasured.

Task T014 writes raw results to 'data/processed/evaluation_runs.csv'. The plan.md and spec.md (Key Entities) define the Single Source of Truth artifact as 'results/raw_evaluations.csv'. This path mismatch creates a traceability gap where downstream tasks (T018+) expecting files in 'results/' may fail or read stale data.
